ELPAC Level 4
Lexile 1300–1400L
DTR · TALK ABOUT A SCENE

Static Never Reached Station Five

STS-MS8-L4-13001400-MSSCI8-QBL-GBL-02 • MS Science Grade 8 • Digitized and Analog Signals
Look at the picture. You will answer some questions about it. Press the play button to hear each question. Record your answer for each question. You can listen to your answer and record it again. Click SUBMIT RECORDINGS when you have answered all the questions.
JUST FOR FUN · OPTIONAL
Five schools on five continents are running the same relay tonight, and the quest only counts if the message that reaches Station 5 is exactly the message that left Station 1. Every team sends it two ways: as a smooth wave and as a string of ones and zeros. Halfway through, the wave arrives crackly and a little wrong, while the ones and zeros arrive exactly as they were sent, every time. That is why almost every message crossing the world tonight travels as digits.
Why does a signal made of ones and zeros survive a long trip better than a smooth wave does?
Optional. You do not have to record this one.
1
What is Amina doing?
Look at the picture. Justify what Amina is doing with precise evidence, and say what she expects her classmates to understand from it.
2
What is Tomas holding?
Look at the picture. Justify what Tomas is holding with precise evidence, and say how it matches something else in the room.
3
What is the teacher doing?
Look at the picture. Justify what the teacher is doing with precise evidence, and say what the labelled machine is for.
4
Where are they? How do you know?
Look at the picture. Justify where they are with two precise details, and say which detail is the strongest proof.
5
Tell me about the signal board.
Look at the picture. Justify what the big board is teaching, and use precise words for how each drawing behaves from end to end.
6
Tell me about what Mei is wearing.
Look at the picture. Justify what Mei is wearing with precise clothing words, and say why that choice suits the work she is doing.
Record an answer for each question, then submit.
Content alignment: NGSS MS-PS4-3 — Digitized and Analog Signals for Encoding and Transmitting Information. ELD (Speaking — official ELPAC “Talk About a Scene”): PI.A.1, PII.B.3, PII.B.4, PII.B.5.
